scope: owner-prop bridge followup on anonymous report/comment nodes against commentInfo targetid/commentid families

cases.label: kids_dualfull_targetid
cases.judgment: Owner-bridge scanning still exposed a feed_id-family identity set rather than the positive commentInfo targetid/commentid family.

cases.label: tv_season_second
cases.judgment: Even on a non-kids positive page, the owner bridge stayed disjoint from commentInfo targetid/commentid and from the observed dokiid family.

cases.label: sports_collection_shell
cases.judgment: The sports positive shell reproduced the same split: owner-bridge ids stayed separate from the single positive commentInfo targetid value.

cases.label: variety_topic_pay15_targetid_uploadsrc
cases.judgment: The negative variety shell stayed negative after the owner-bridge followup as well; no convergence with commentInfo targetid/commentid emerged.

takeaways: Even the added dataset and owner-prop bridge scan around anonymous report/comment nodes still did not surface the same targetid/commentid values from the positive commentInfo family.

On pages where root.base.commentInfo.targetid/commentid is non-empty, the sampled comment report buttons still expose a different identity family (feed_id / cp_id / counters) rather than the decoded commentInfo targetid/commentid values.
The anonymous comment-report DOM identity tags also stay disjoint from the currently observed dokiid/ftid discussion chain on the sampled positive pages.

This narrows the natural-producer gap: in the tested anonymous DOM path, commentInfo targetid/commentid survives as a neighboring payload family, but the sampled report-button producer surface still does not expose or consume those same ids directly.
current_judgment: The owner-bridge followup upgrades the anonymous report-button negative evidence from plain dt-params audit to shallow framework-owner audit. The tested PC Web natural path still stays inside a feed_id/cp_id identity family rather than the commentInfo targetid/commentid family.
